features.title: Update header text in Next.js layout or landing page
features.description: Locate the component responsible for rendering the header text (likely `app/src/app/layout.tsx` or `app/src/app/page.tsx`) and change the header text to 'Intentional Industries'. Ensure the change is visible in the UI and does not break existing functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: The text 'Intentional Industries' appears in the header of the application
Running `grep -r 'Intentional Industries' app/src/app/` returns at least one match
The application builds successfully with `cd app && npm run build` (exit code 0)
No TypeScript compilation errors when running `cd app && npx tsc --noEmit`
The layout remains structurally intact (no broken HTML or missing elements)

features.title: Rebuild Docker image with updated header text
features.description: Build a new Docker image containing the header text change using the existing multi-stage Dockerfile. Tag the image appropriately for pushing to ECR. Verify the build completes without errors and the image contains the updated code.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `docker build -t test-header-change:latest app/` completes with exit code 0
The Docker image is created and visible in `docker images` output
Starting a container from the image with `docker run -p 3000:3000 test-header-change:latest` serves the application
The build process completes in under 10 minutes

features.title: Deploy updated application to AWS infrastructure
features.description: Push the newly built Docker image to ECR and trigger a redeployment of the ECS service. This can be done by running the full `./up` script or manually pushing to ECR and updating the ECS task definition. Wait for the deployment to stabilize and the health checks to pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `./up` completes successfully with exit code 0
The ECS service shows 'RUNNING' status with desired count matching running count
The ALB target group health check returns 'healthy' status
CloudWatch logs for the ECS task show the application started without errors
The deployment completes within 15 minutes

features.title: Verify header text change in live deployment
features.description: Access the deployed application via the CloudFront URL and verify that the header text now reads 'Intentional Industries'. Check both the homepage and any other pages that display the header. Capture a screenshot or use curl to verify the HTML contains the expected text.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `curl -s $(terraform -chdir=terraform output -raw cloudfront_url) | grep 'Intentional Industries'` returns a match
Manually accessing the CloudFront URL in a browser shows 'Intentional Industries' in the header
The homepage (`/`) displays the header text correctly
The profile page (`/profile`) displays the same header text
No console errors appear in the browser developer tools

features.title: Run acceptance test suite to ensure no regressions
features.description: Execute the full Playwright acceptance test suite (`./test`) to verify that the header text change has not broken any existing functionality. All 5 tests (homepage, API, signup, signin, profile) should pass. If any tests fail due to the header change, update the test expectations accordingly.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `./test` completes with exit code 0
All 5 tests in `acceptance/specs/v1-acceptance.spec.ts` pass
No new test failures are introduced by the header change
The test output shows 'All tests passed' or equivalent success message
Test screenshots in `acceptance/screenshots/` show the updated header text
